items.title: Lightning Round: Stay long Primoris Solutions, says Jim Cramer
items.description: 'Mad Money' host Jim Cramer weighs in on stocks including: EOG Resources, American Express, Primoris Services, and more.

items.title: Mastercard at 25.9X P/E: Pricey, But Not Overpriced - Buy or Wait?
items.description: MA trades at 25.9X earnings, above peers but below its historical norm. Strong cross-border growth and services expansion raise the question: buy now or wait?

items.title: When does a 0% APR credit card offer make sense? 3 things to know before you apply
items.description: If you have the credit to qualify and you can pay off the balance, an intro APR can save you hundreds of dollars or more.

items.title: Is American Express (AXP) Still Attractively Priced After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run
items.description: If you are wondering whether American Express is still priced reasonably after its long run, this article walks through what the current share price might be implying about value. The stock closed at US$305.38, with a 7 day return of 0.7% decline, a 30 day return of 15.0% decline and a year to date return of 18.1% decline. The 1 year, 3 year and 5 year returns sit at 18.1%, 100.8% and 120.7% respectively. Recent price moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing focus on how American Express...

items.title: Investing $1K in Warren Buffett’s Favorite Bank Stocks 10 Years Ago Would Have Netted This Much
items.description: American Express (NYSE: AXP) and Bank of America (NYSE: BAC) are two of Warren Buffett’s most enduring financial bets. Both have been cornerstones of Berkshire Hathaway’s portfolio for years, built on his conviction in durable business models, pricing power, and consistent capital returns. Holding either stock is really a story of trusting that thesis through ... Investing $1K in Warren Buffett’s Favorite Bank Stocks 10 Years Ago Would Have Netted This Much
